conversations.content:
I'll be arriving in London on March 15th. Last year, I stayed at the 'London Luxury Hotel', which had amazing reviews and ratings. It was super quiet and clean, and my room had a view of the Thames River. It was such a great experience! This time, I want to try out a new hotel. Once I figure out which one is the best, I'll head there and then find a good restaurant to eat at with my friends.
I have a budget of 160 for the hotel, and I want to stay in a hotel with the highest rating. Can you recommend a hotel? Tell me the name, rating, price range, and address. Thanks a bunch!

<function_thought>
To fulfill the user's request, I need to gather all hotels in London first and then filter them based on the user's budget of 160. After that, I will look for the highest-rated hotel among the collected hotels and provide its name, rating, price range, and address. Following the initialized function trajectory, I will begin with the `get_all_hotels_in_city` function.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[get_all_hotels_in_city(city="London")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

Hotel Names: Cozy Stay
Mom's Home
London Luxury
Covent Garden Retreat
Riverside View

<function_thought>
The hotels retrieved in London are: "Cozy Stay", "Mom's Home", "London Luxury", "Covent Garden Retreat", and "Riverside View". The next step is to get the prices for these hotels so I can filter them based on the user's budget of 160.
</function_thought>
